classification.reasoning: The paper contains multiple ablation tables that systematically vary design choices (consistent sampling, shared noise, attention sink, stride strategies, sequence length) and report metrics (CD, IoU, F-Score, PFLOPs). There are well over 10 extractable quantitative data points with clear table citations.

main_claims: Sculpt4D's block sparse attention mechanism reduces total network computation by 56% compared to full attention without compromising 4D generation quality.
Sculpt4D achieves state-of-the-art results on 4D generation benchmarks in terms of geometric quality (Chamfer Distance, IoU, F-Score) compared to L4GM, V2M4, and GVFD.
The sparse attention mechanism's computational advantage scales favorably with sequence length, dropping to 21.5% of full attention FLOPs at the layer level for 32-frame sequences.
Sculpt4D generalizes to sequences up to 4x the training length (64 frames) with only marginal degradation in geometric metrics.
Sculpt4D achieves substantially lower FVD than competing methods while requiring only 7 minutes of inference time.

metrics.name: total_flops_ratio
metrics.label: Total Network FLOPs Ratio vs Full Attention

metrics.name: layer_flops_ratio
metrics.label: Sparse Attention Layer FLOPs Ratio vs Full Attention
